human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for lending-servicing boundary discipline, boarding and payment evidence quality, payoff and transfer handling, suspicious-activity escalation posture, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to loan boarding, payment application, suspense and unapplied-funds handling, payoff support, servicing transfer, and evidence-backed exception routing.
Authority sources cover CFPB servicing rules, Fannie Mae servicing workflow guidance, FFIEC lending-risk guidance, MISMO transfer and boarding standards, and Nacha ACH exception controls.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing prerequisites, unsupported overrides, ACH return rework, suspicious payoff escalation, borrower-impact conflicts, terminology fidelity, low-confidence escalation, public-source conflict, tenant adaptation, and adjacent-lane routing.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/financial-services/lending-operations-specialist/ so lending operations stays separate from underwriting, fraud, AML, legal, collections, and loss-mitigation lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around payment-crediting overrides, payoff completion, suspicious payment handling, borrower-impact communication, and policy conflicts.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated lending operations specialist for evidence-backed loan-servicing execution, transfer handling, payment operations, and exception routing.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
